packageRules.description: OpenTelemetry: update core (v1.x) and the 0.x log modules together in ONE PR and never automerge - a core bump without the log modules (or vice-versa) breaks the build. Repo rules run after the fleet-wide automerge in config.js, so this overrides it for these packages.
Ignore the self-module reference: the tools/* modules require github.com/rknightion/tailscale2otel but replace ../.. makes Go always build from local code, so the require version is cosmetic.

Update indirect deps in the tools/* modules. They pull the whole build list in transitively via replace ../.., so every dep except the self-module is indirect there - and Renovate skips indirect deps by default. A root bump of a shared dep would otherwise leave tools/*/go.mod pinned to the old version, and Go then refuses to build them ('updates to go.mod needed'). Enabling this puts both sides of the bump in the same PR.

...but never raise a standalone MAJOR upgrade of one of those indirect deps. In Go a major is a distinct module path, so backoff/v5 and /v7 (or yaml/v2 and /v3) legitimately coexist in the build list because different deps require each. Renovate reads the older line as upgradable, rewrites it to the newer path, and emits a DUPLICATE require entry - an invalid go.mod that fails with 'updates to go.mod needed' (this broke #158 and #159). Indirect requires are MVS output: they may only move in lockstep with a root bump, never be upgraded on their own.
